steps.title: Find the small height
steps.content:
In right triangle $ABC$, use the Pythagorean theorem with hypotenuse $$\sqrt{89}$$ and leg $8$.
$$BC^2+8^2=(\sqrt{89})^2$$
$$BC^2+64=89$$
$$BC=5$$

steps.title: Scale the height
steps.content:
The full base is
$$AE=AC+CE=8+24=32$$
The height-to-base ratio is
$$\frac{BC}{AC}=\frac{5}{8}$$
So the larger height is
$$DE=\frac{5}{8}(32)=20$$

steps.title: Compute the area
steps.content: Use the triangle area formula with base $32$ and height $20$. $$\frac{1}{2}(32)(20)=320$$ The answer is 320.
